scenes.text: 118-bus power system, 10 zones. Generation must meet load, and reserves must protect against correlated forecast uncertainty.
Forecast errors have structure. A diagonal box wastes capacity on unlikely directions. Our learned shape is tighter where it counts.
Each zone sees uncertainty through an exposure direction. The reserve requirement is the support function: worst-case reach of the uncertainty shape.
Reserves are not free. The system rebalances around them. Shadow prices reveal where protection hurts the objective the most.
The gradient flows backward. Dual multipliers from constrained zones reshape the uncertainty set to reduce downstream cost.
Over training, the ellipsoid shrinks. Cost drops $4.5K/hr, reserves drop 47%, while maintaining 96.7% coverage.
Four methods, same data. At every coverage level, the learned method achieves lower operating cost.
